purpose: Ministry-wise catalog of every company-facing incentive policy this program tracks, VERIFIED AGAINST EACH MINISTRY'S OWN WEBSITE (plus the NSWS gateway) by six parallel agents on 2026-07-20. For each ministry: active schemes with what companies actually get, application windows open right now, closed/legacy schemes, and the health of the ministry's own scheme pages. ~100 policies across 16 ministries + NSWS.
method: Six agents each covering a ministry cluster; verification via direct fetch of ministry sites, their headless CMS APIs where the public site is a JS shell, dedicated scheme portals, and rendered-browser passes; NSWS (nsws.gov.in) verified in-session. Counts, rates and dates are as stated on the government pages fetched; 'not published'/'not verifiable' recorded where that is the truth. Full per-policy detail (URLs, notes) preserved below.
headlines: ACCESS ARCHITECTURE: the ministry web estate has quietly gone headless -- MeitY, DoT and DPIIT are client-rendered shells whose real content lives in public WordPress JSON APIs (site.gov.in/cms/wp-json/...), and commerce.gov.in's content API answers to the Basic-auth header shipped in its own JS bundle. Meanwhile three domains are dead or unreachable (fert.nic.in and texmin.nic.in DNS-dead, mines.gov.in timing out), msme.gov.in's scheme list never renders even in a browser, and six scheme pages are provably stale (ACC re-tender absent, steel PLI frozen at Mar 2025 under a fresh footer stamp, MNRE PLI last updated Aug 2023, MoPNG still citing '20% by 2030').

OPEN RIGHT NOW (as of 20 Jul 2026): UNNATI 2024 registration to 30-Sep-2026; Coal Gasification 2026 Round-1 (RFP 05-Jun, pre-application conference 16-Jul); DoP's API-CF and SMDI calls (23-Jun/03-Jul-2026); MoFPI cold-chain irradiation EoI + PM-FME + Operation Greens claims; Steel R&D proposals; AHIDF and NLM loan portals; ECMS window (extended 30-Jul-2025); ALMM enlistment (re-opened May 2026); PM E-DRIVE e-2W claims to 31-Jul-2026.

VERIFIED CLOSURES beyond the scheme registry: SPECS and EMC 2.0 both closed to applications 31-Mar-2024; ATUFS closed (claims settlement only); Interest Equalisation Scheme lapsed 31-Dec-2024 (successor: NIRYAT PROTSAHAN subvention); IFLDP application portal closed (NSWS notice) at its Rs 1,700cr window's end; M-SIPS and original EMC are maintained legacy pages.

CORRECTION APPLIED to the scheme registry: PM E-DRIVE was extended segment-wise (e-2W to 31-Jul-2026, e-rickshaws/carts to 31-Mar-2028; only L5 closed 26-Dec-2025) -- the live portal contradicts the announced two-year window.

nsws_gateway.what: National Single Window System (nsws.gov.in) -- the DPIIT/Invest India application gateway: 32 ministries and 34 States/UTs live; last reviewed 07-Jul-2026. Its public 'Government Schemes' catalog curates six scheme gateways (Vehicle Scrapping Policy, Ethanol Policy + Helpdesk, IFLDP, Solar PV PLI, National Green Hydrogen Mission, PM GatiShakti), each with About/documents/links/contacts and an 'Apply for Scheme Related Approvals' flow into the Know-Your-Approvals engine.
unique_contributions: IFLDP (DPIIT, leather/footwear): Rs 1,700cr to 31-Mar-2026, seven sub-schemes with concrete rates -- CETP assistance 70% (80% NE) capped Rs 200cr; IDLS modernization subsidy 30% MSME / 20% others (40%/30% NE); mega-cluster assistance 50% (70% NE) capped Rs 125cr; brand promotion Rs 100cr -- a company-facing scheme absent from every ministry-cluster brief
Formal 'Notice for closure of NSWS portal for Submission of Applications under IFLDP 2021-26' -- an application-window closure the ministry-side pages never surface
NSWS is itself the durable verification target for application-window status: scheme pages carry validated documents and the apply flow shows whether a window is live
nsws_gateway.access_note: Outer site is server-rendered Drupal (curl-friendly); /portal/* scheme pages are a React SPA needing a browser. No public API for a full scheme registry -- the catalog is 6 curated gateways, not comprehensive.

cross_cutting_findings: The verification layer is now load-bearing: five flagship schemes' ministry pages contradict reality (ACC re-tender, steel Round-1.2/withdrawals, E-DRIVE extension, FAME-II closure, EBP targets) -- Cabinet releases and live portals beat ministry scheme pages for currency, which is exactly why the registry tracks PRIDs and portals, not pages.

Headless-CMS access pattern (site.gov.in/cms/wp-json) is the single most useful discovery for the quarterly refresh: MeitY, DoT and DPIIT all serve complete, dated scheme content through it while their public sites render nothing to curl.

Application windows cluster in capex/cluster schemes (DoP SPI/SMDI, MoFPI, gasification, UNNATI) while PLI windows are almost all closed -- consistent with the five-year instrument migration: the state is currently BUYING capacity and infrastructure, not output.

Three central-government domains are dead or dark (fert.nic.in, texmin.nic.in, mines.gov.in) and one ministry's scheme list is unrenderable (msme.gov.in) -- for four of 16 ministries the official web estate cannot answer 'what schemes do you run'.

NSWS is the best single cross-ministry surface for application-window truth (it carried the only formal IFLDP closure notice found anywhere) but its catalog covers only 6 scheme gateways -- it is a gateway, not a registry.
